text: Managing a team effectively requires strong communication, delegation, feedback, and motivation skills. Good communication means clearly explaining tasks and expectations to everyone. Delegation involves sharing work based on each person’s strengths, which builds trust and efficiency. Regular feedback helps team members improve and feel valued—praise achievements and offer constructive suggestions. Motivation keeps the team engaged; recognise efforts and set achievable goals. When conflicts arise, address them calmly by listening to all sides and finding a fair solution. Ultimately, balancing these elements creates a positive, productive environment where everyone works together successfully.
